2026-07-12.patch_summary: This patch adds a new helper function called memory_block_aligned_range() to the Linux kernel's mm/memory module. The function takes an input range and returns a new range that is aligned to memory block boundaries, which is necessary for memory hotplug operations. The function also guards against end underflow by returning an empty range if the input range falls below the first memory block boundary. The patch updates the dax/kmem driver to use this new helper function.
